market_context: Quarter-end, AI-narrative-driven tape. Bear case: Benzinga 07-01 'Apollo's Top Economist Issues Grim Warning As AI Boom Risks a Painful Repricing' and 'Michael Burry Reveals Tesla, Nvidia Shorts, Says Chip Boom Is Beginning of the End'. Offset by 06-30 'Nasdaq 100 Soars As Semis Rebound' and 07-01 'Nasdaq Jumps 1.5%, Records Sharp Quarterly Gains'. Mag 7 names sit well off 30-day highs (MSFT 372.92 vs 460; NVDA 199.93 vs 224; GOOGL 357.31 vs 396.8); JNJ (253.97) and UNH (415.42) grind near range highs. Default is hold across tech; the one action is a modest JNJ continuation buy on an extended clinical/regulatory catalyst stream. Portfolio is 100% cash, zero open positions (manifest holdings empty), so no holdings news sweep required. DATA CAVEAT: yfinance fundamentals failed for all 7 tickers (network policy blocks fc.yahoo.com); theses rest on fresh Alpaca prices (30 bars each) and Benzinga headlines only.

signals.thesis: 06-12 buy thesis intact and extended: 06-26 TECVAYLI got a positive CHMP opinion for EU approval in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, and IMAAVY posted durable Week-24 MG-ADL reductions across 12 EAN 2026 abstracts. Close 253.97 is up ~11% from the 238 base and holds the top of the 30-day range (222.9-258.4); entry is less attractive than three weeks ago (chasing risk noted). Falsified if close < 240, the CHMP opinion reverses at EC, or talc litigation escalates materially.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-26: J&J's TECVAYLI Receives CHMP Approval Recommendation In Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma
Benzinga 2026-06-26: J&J Announces Additional IMAAVY Data Across 12 Abstracts At EAN 2026; Greater MG-ADL Reductions At Week 24

signals.thesis: No clean catalyst, conflicting signals. Benzinga 06-30 'Epic Games, Apple Clash Reaches Supreme Court In Battle Over App Store Payment Rules' reopens fee risk; 07-01 'Tim Cook Holds Constructive Talks With EU Tech Chief Over Siri AI' is positive but talk-stage. Close 288.87 sits mid-low in the 30-day range (275.2-315.2), below the 06-12 level of 295. No earnings or shipping-product trigger. Hold; re-evaluate on a concrete App Store ruling or a shipped Siri AI feature.

signals.thesis: Weak drift, cost-side headlines, no actionable trigger. Benzinga 07-01 'Microsoft to Announce Fresh Layoffs Impacting Thousands as AI Investment Surge Reshapes Workforce' is ambiguous; 07-01 'Energy Secretary Says Data Center Fears Are Overblown' cuts against the capex-glut worry. Close 372.92 sits deep in the 30-day range (352.4-460.5), below the 06-12 level of 390. Neither a clean buy nor sell; hold, re-evaluate if layoffs signal a real margin inflection or capex guidance shifts.

Close 357.31 is well off the 30-day high (396.8), near the low (335.8). No clean trigger; hold, re-evaluate on antitrust remedy news or measurable Gemini monetization.

signals.thesis: Grinding near range highs on analyst-chatter only. Today's snapshot headlines are all price-target grade: Benzinga 06-30 'Morgan Stanley Maintains Overweight, Raises PT to $468' and 06-24 'B of A Maintains Buy, Raises PT to $475' — exactly the chatter the routine ignores. No hard operational or regulatory catalyst; the 06-11 Medicare Advantage watchdog concern has no fresh follow-up. Close 415.42 near the 30-day high (427.4). Chasing range highs on no news has poor risk/reward; hold.
sources: Benzinga 2026-06-30: Morgan Stanley Maintains Overweight on UnitedHealth Group, Raises Price Target to $468 (price-target chatter, noted not relied upon)
